title: Always True
description: Learn how to talk about things that happen every day or things that are always true.
lesson_content_markdown:
# Always True (Present Simple)

We use the **Present Simple** tense to speak about three main things:

### 1. Facts (Always True)

These are things that do not change.

- The library **opens** at 8 AM every day.
- The sun **rises** in the east.
- Water **boils** at 100 degrees Celsius.

### 2. Habits (Daily Life)

These are things that you do many times. 

- I **go** to the cafe every morning.
- Students **submit** their homework on Fridays.
- My teacher **speaks** English very well.

### 3. Rules and Systems

- The bus **stops** near the school.
- The gym **closes** at 9 PM on weekends.

### How to build the sentence:

**I, You, We, They**
- **Action**: No change to the word.
- **Example**: I **study** math.

**He, She, It (or one person/thing)**
- **Action**: Add **-s** or **-es**.
- **Example**: He **studies** math.

**Important Note:** When the subject is ONE person or thing (like "My friend" or "The library"), we MUST add **-s**.

- **I study** (0% change) 
- **My friend studies** (+s change) 
- **The buses arrive** (Plural - 0% change) 
- **The bus arrives** (Singular - +s change) 

---

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: Fill in the blank with the correct form of the verb in the brackets (). Use the Present Simple.

questions.question: The history teacher ___ (talk) very fast in every class.
We ___ (buy) coffee in the morning before the lesson.
The library ___ (open) at 9:00 AM.
I ___ (read) a new chapter every night.
My friends ___ (go) to the gym after school.
The professor ___ (give) a test every Friday.
Students ___ (clean) their desks at the end of class.
She ___ (like) her English class very much.
The bus ___ (stop) in front of the gate.
You ___ (speak) English very well.
He ___ (write) his homework with a black pen.
The sun ___ (shine) into the windows of the room.
They ___ (have) three classes on Monday.
The food in the cafe ___ (taste) like home.
I ___ (want) to be a doctor one day.
The music at the party ___ (sound) very loud.
We ___ (walk) to the library together.
The boy ___ (carry) a heavy bag to the room.
My father ___ (work) near the university.
The cat ___ (sleep) under the tree.
They ___ (drink) water during the game.
The girl ___ (sing) for the music club.
He ___ (need) more time to finish the book.
I ___ (know) your sister very well.
The winter ___ (bring) cold snow to the campus.
We ___ (understand) the teacher's lesson.
The clock on the wall ___ (tick) loudly.
She ___ (play) the piano in the student center.
The class ___ (begin) at 10:00 every Tuesday.
They ___ (live) in the new student dorm.
